sae_features.label: IgV CDR-like loops
sae_features.description: N-terminal V-set (IgV) immunoglobulin-like domains of IgSF cell-surface receptors/adhesion molecules and viral Ig-like mimics, with strongest signal over the ligand-binding CDR-like loops and adjacent beta-strands of the beta-sandwich, including the canonical disulfide-forming cysteines.

sae_features.label: Immunoglobulin-like ectodomains
sae_features.description: Extracellular/luminal immunoglobulin-like beta-sandwich modules (IgSF V-, C1- and C2-set) and immediately adjacent inter-domain linkers in secretory and cell-surface proteins; the feature captures the Ig-like ectodomain scaffold used for adhesion and immune recognition, with weaker spillover to related FN3 beta-sandwich repeats.

sae_features.label: Type I/GPI IgSF receptors
sae_features.description: Type I single-pass or GPI-anchored cell-surface glycoprotein architecture—especially IgSF receptors/adhesion molecules and viral mimics—recognized via their membrane-anchoring elements: strong focus on the C-terminal single transmembrane helix plus adjacent disordered cytoplasmic tail, or alternatively the C-terminal GPI signal; secondary recognition of the N-terminal signal peptide; the folded extracellular Ig-like ectodomains are largely ignored

sae_features.label: N-terminal Sec-dependent signal peptide
sae_features.description: N-terminal Sec-dependent signal peptide, specifically the hydrophobic helical h‑region (and adjacent c‑region near the cleavage site) of secreted/single-pass cell-surface proteins, notably immunoglobulin-superfamily receptors and IgCAMs

sae_features.label: IgV ligand-binding face
sae_features.description: Ligand-binding face of N-terminal immunoglobulin V-set domains in extracellular IgSF glycoproteins—specifically the C-terminal half of the IgV fold (GFCC' sheet and FG region) used for receptor–ligand recognition and often bearing N-linked glycosylation sequons and the intradomain disulfide cysteine

sae_features.label: N-terminal ectodomain cap
sae_features.description: N-terminal “mature ectodomain cap” immediately after the signal peptide in secreted/cell‑surface glycoproteins—specifically the first disulfide‑bonded module of extracellular domains (e.g., Ig‑like V‑set, LU/Ly6/uPAR, TGF‑β receptor cysteine‑rich regions), enriched in early β‑strands/loops with conserved cysteines and frequent N‑glycosylation sequons; avoids the signal peptide core, transmembrane segment, and cytosolic tail

sae_features.label: Type I cytosolic tails
sae_features.description: Cytosolic C-terminal tails of single-pass type I membrane (and viral envelope) proteins, especially the juxtamembrane, intrinsically disordered segments enriched in acidic/Ser/Thr/Pro residues and tyrosine-based endocytic/signaling motifs (NPxY/YENPTY), which mediate adaptor binding, phosphorylation, and trafficking.

sae_features.label: Type I single-pass transmembrane helix
sae_features.description: Single-pass type I membrane-protein transmembrane alpha-helix (stop-transfer/signal-anchor) that anchors large ectodomains of metazoan cell-surface receptors/adhesion molecules and analogous viral envelope glycoproteins
